predictions.text: By 2026-04-30, the U.S. (DoD/CENTCOM) or UK (MoD/Royal Navy) will publicly announce a specific naval escort/convoy or enhanced-protection operation for commercial shipping transiting the Strait of Hormuz or Gulf of Oman (named operation or clearly defined mission).
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if an official U.S. or UK government/military press release, briefing transcript, or official social-media statement announces a defined escort/convoy/protection operation (beyond generic patrols) for merchant vessels in/near the Strait of Hormuz or Gulf of Oman on/before 2026-04-30.
evidence: Structured summary highlights elevated Hormuz disruption risk and broader Iran-war escalation dynamics.
monitoring_signals: CENTCOM press releases and briefings
UK Ministry of Defence/Royal Navy announcements
UKMTO advisories that reference escorts or coalition protection
Statements from major shipping associations (ICS, BIMCO) citing escorts
predictions.rationale: If risk to commercial shipping rises, governments often shift from advisories to explicit escort/protection missions to stabilize trade flows.

predictions.text: By 2026-05-31, at least one top-20 U.S. airport operator will publicly warn passengers of longer TSA security wait times specifically due to the DHS funding lapse/shutdown (or related TSA staffing/pay disruption).
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if an official airport statement (press release, airport website alert, or verified social-media post) from a top-20 U.S. airport by annual passengers explicitly attributes longer TSA/security lines to the DHS funding lapse/shutdown or TSA staffing/pay disruption, on/before 2026-05-31.
evidence: Structured summary notes TSA disruption and airport friction during the partial shutdown.
monitoring_signals: Airport official social channels and newsroom pages (ATL, LAX, ORD, DFW, DEN, JFK, etc.)
Local TV news quoting airport management statements
TSA public statements about staffing and screening times
predictions.rationale: Airports commonly issue proactive advisories when screening capacity is impaired, especially during widely reported funding disruptions.

predictions.text: On or before 2026-06-30, the S&P 500 will have at least one trading day with a close-to-close decline of 3.0% or more.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if any trading day on/before 2026-06-30 has (S&P 500 close - prior close) / prior close <= -3.0%, using official index close values.
evidence: Structured summary describes conflict-driven risk-off moves and heightened risk premia.
monitoring_signals: Daily S&P 500 percent change
Intraday volatility spikes and VIX term structure steepening
Major conflict escalation headlines tied to Iran/Hormuz
predictions.rationale: Geopolitical shocks and oil-risk repricing increase the probability of single-day equity drawdowns.

predictions.text: By 2026-06-30, the European Commission will publish an official update on its cyberattack that explicitly states whether data exfiltration occurred (e.g., 'no evidence' vs 'data accessed/exfiltrated'), and if yes, names at least one affected directorate/service or system.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if an EC official communication (press release, spokesperson briefing, official Q&A, or published incident update) explicitly addresses data exfiltration status and, if exfiltration/unauthorized access is confirmed, names at least one impacted EC service/directorate or information system, on/before 2026-06-30.
evidence: Structured summary notes the European Commission confirmed a cyberattack after breach claims.
monitoring_signals: European Commission press corner and spokesperson briefings
EDPS (EU data protection supervisor) statements
CERT-EU advisories referencing EC incident
predictions.rationale: High-profile institutional breaches usually prompt follow-up disclosures after initial triage and forensic scoping.

predictions.text: By 2026-07-31, CISA will publish a public advisory/guidance document where the title or executive summary explicitly mentions 'generative AI' or 'LLM(s)' as a factor in phishing/social engineering threat activity.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if a CISA web-posted advisory/guidance/analysis report on/before 2026-07-31 has 'generative AI' or 'LLM'/'large language model' explicitly in the title or executive summary and ties it to phishing or social engineering tactics.
evidence: Structured summary notes heightened concern about more powerful frontier models and shifting cyber attack/defense balance.
monitoring_signals: CISA advisories and 'News & Events' posts
Joint Cybersecurity Advisories (CISA/FBI/NSA)
Phishing campaign reporting that highlights AI-assisted lures
predictions.rationale: As AI-assisted social engineering becomes routine, CISA tends to formalize the risk in guidance for critical infrastructure and government networks.

predictions.text: By 2026-08-31, at least one U.S. state public utility commission (PUC) will open a formal docket (or equivalent proceeding) specifically focused on data center/AI-driven electricity load growth and cost allocation/interconnection impacts.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if any state PUC posts an order/notice opening a new docket (or initiating a specific proceeding) where the stated purpose includes data centers and/or AI-related large loads and addresses planning, interconnection, tariffs, or cost allocation, on/before 2026-08-31.
evidence: Structured summary highlights grid strain and energy-security tradeoffs amid rising AI/data-center demand concerns.
monitoring_signals: PUC docket search pages in high data-center-growth states (VA, OH, TX, AZ, GA, NC, IA, OR)
Utility IRP filings referencing data center/AI load
Regional grid operator warnings about large-load queues
predictions.rationale: Rapid large-load additions are increasingly triggering regulatory proceedings on who pays for upgrades and how quickly interconnections can be approved.

predictions.text: By 2026-12-31, FERC will hold a technical conference OR issue a formal notice (e.g., NOPR, NOI, or order initiating inquiry) explicitly focused on reliability/planning challenges from large new loads such as data centers (including AI workloads).
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if FERC, on/before 2026-12-31, (a) holds a technical conference whose agenda/notice explicitly references data centers/large loads/AI load growth and reliability/planning, or (b) issues a NOPR/NOI/order initiating inquiry explicitly on that topic.
evidence: Structured summary discusses grid constraints and the energy transition’s vulnerability amid rising electricity-demand anxiety.
monitoring_signals: FERC eLibrary for 'large load', 'data center', 'AI' keywords
FERC meeting agendas and technical-conference notices
NERC reliability assessments citing large-load growth
predictions.rationale: If large-load growth continues to accelerate, federal reliability and interconnection policy attention is likely to formalize through conferences or rulemaking steps.

predictions.text: By 2026-12-31, OpenAI will publicly confirm an IPO process milestone by either (a) filing an S-1 publicly, or (b) announcing it has confidentially submitted a draft registration statement to the SEC.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if on/before 2026-12-31 OpenAI (or its parent entity) has a publicly available SEC S-1 filing, or OpenAI issues an official statement that it has confidentially submitted a draft registration statement for an IPO to the SEC.
evidence: Structured summary notes reporting that large financing is interpreted as positioning for a 2026 OpenAI IPO.
monitoring_signals: SEC EDGAR searches for OpenAI-related registrants
OpenAI official blog/newsroom and major-wire confirmations (Reuters, WSJ, FT)
Underwriter/lead-left bank reporting tied to IPO preparation
predictions.rationale: Large-scale financing and infrastructure buildout can precede formal IPO steps, but timing remains uncertain and dependent on market and regulatory conditions.
